title: Exponent Elevates John Pye to President and Eric Anderson to CFO Reporting to Catherine Corrigan, CEO; Rich Schlenker to Remain as Executive VP and to Stand for Election to Board of Directors
author: Na
description: Exponent, Inc. announced executive leadership changes effective May 1, 2026, promoting John Pye to President and Eric Anderson to Chief Financial Officer, both reporting to CEO Catherine Corrigan. Richard Schlenker will transition from CFO to Executive Vice President and stand for Board election. Paul Johnston will retire from the Board, with Karen Richardson becoming Chairman at the June 4, 2026 Annual Meeting.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The announcement reflects strong internal talent development with promotions of experienced executives who have deep institutional knowledge and proven track records. The leadership transitions are orderly and well-planned, with continuity maintained through experienced leaders. The company also increased its quarterly dividend from $0.30 to $0.31 per share, signaling financial strength and confidence in future performance.

title: Exponent Reports Fourth Quarter and Fiscal Year 2025 Financial Results
author: Globe Newswire
description: Exponent (NASDAQ: EXPO) reported strong Q4 2025 results with total revenues increasing 7.8% to $147.4 million and net income rising to $24.8 million ($0.49 per diluted share). For fiscal year 2025, revenues grew 4.2% to $582.0 million with net income of $106.0 million ($2.07 per diluted share). The company increased its quarterly dividend from $0.30 to $0.31 and provided guidance for FY2026 expecting high-single-digit revenue growth with EBITDA margins of 27.6% to 28.1%.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong Q4 and FY2025 results with revenue growth of 7.8% in Q4 and 4.2% for the full year, improved net income, increased EBITDA margins, dividend increase, and positive forward guidance for FY2026 with expected high-single-digit revenue growth. Management expressed confidence in market opportunities and long-term growth trajectory.

title: Exponent Increases Quarterly Dividend Payment from $0.30 to $0.31 per Share for Q1 2026
author: Globe Newswire
description: Exponent Inc. (NASDAQ: EXPO) announced that its Board of Directors has increased its quarterly cash dividend from $0.30 to $0.31 per share, effective for Q1 2026. The dividend will be paid on March 20, 2026 to shareholders of record as of March 6, 2026. CEO Dr. Catherine Corrigan attributed the increase to strong market drivers and confidence in the company's scientific and engineering capabilities.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company increased its quarterly dividend by approximately 3.3% (from $0.30 to $0.31), demonstrating confidence in future cash flows and commitment to returning capital to shareholders. The CEO's commentary about strong market drivers and confidence in the business further supports a positive outlook.

title: Independent Investment Manager Sells $24.6 Million of Exponent Stock, According to Recent Filing
author: Jake Lerch
description: Conestoga Capital Advisors sold 344,631 shares of Exponent, valued at $24.63 million, reducing its stake from 2.99% to 2.63% of its portfolio in Q3 2025. The sale comes amid Exponent's underperformance compared to the S&P 500.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock has underperformed S&P 500 by over 50 percentage points year-to-date, and the investment manager reduced its position, indicating reduced confidence in the company's near-term prospects
Mentioned as a top holding in the fund, but no specific performance details were provided

title: Exponent (EXPO) Q2 Revenue Rises 1%
author: Jesterai
description: Exponent reported a modest 1% revenue increase to $142.0 million in Q2 FY2025, with declining profitability due to rising operating costs. The company saw stable performance driven by reactive consulting services, particularly in construction and medical devices, while experiencing softness in proactive services.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Mixed financial performance with slight revenue growth (1%) but declining profitability (EPS down 8.8%), stable reactive services, but weakness in proactive segments
